# You Owe Me

You Owe Me is an iPhone app for tracking money between real people. It helps with IOUs, shared expenses, running balances, temporary financial support, Balance Replay, Relationship Timeline, Loan Records, payment plans, repayments, partial repayments, recurring costs, family reimbursements, roommate bills, couple spending, reminders, Live Links, PDF statements, and calm money conversations.

The app is not only a loan tracker. It is a relationship-safe running balance app for money between people.
You Owe Me is not a lender. It does not provide loans, approve credit, advance cash, process payments, or connect users with lenders.
It is also not a bank, debt collector, payment processor, cash advance provider, or accounting system.

Core concept:
A running balance between two people is one current total that updates after each expense, repayment, partial repayment, or adjustment. Balance Replay lets users scroll through entry history and see the balance around the entry being viewed, including interest-aware historical context. Relationship Timeline is the per-person history that keeps entries, loans, payment plans, shared statements, exports, follow-ups, repayment updates, and loan requests in context. You Owe Me is built for ongoing money between real people where the balance, history, reminders, and next conversation need to stay clear.

App Store:
https://apps.apple.com/us/app/loan-tracker-you-owe-me/id1147058670

Family reimbursements App Store custom product page:
https://apps.apple.com/us/app/loan-tracker-you-owe-me/id1147058670?ppid=bc366b6c-90ff-4cde-9ae7-d420c6512e7a

Roommate expenses App Store custom product page:
https://apps.apple.com/us/app/loan-tracker-you-owe-me/id1147058670?ppid=18039f2b-da9e-4d5f-9ba1-b60f117ecf12

## Core pages

- [Home](https://you-owe-me.com/)
- [Find your money situation](https://you-owe-me.com/find/): A situation router that helps visitors choose the right You Owe Me guide, tool, solution, feature, or tracker for money owed, Group Paybacks, repayments, family reimbursements, roommate costs, temporary support, running balances, and shared expenses.
- [Features](https://you-owe-me.com/features/): Deeper feature overview covering running balances, Split Entry, Group Paybacks, Balance Replay, Relationship Timeline, Loan Records, Smart Money Check-Ins, Money Conversations, Live Link, reminders, recurring entries, PDF statements, interest tracking, and offline use.
- [Quick Start Guide](https://you-owe-me.com/quick-start/): Lightweight guide explaining how YouOweMe works: one running balance per person, entries, repayments, Split Entry, Loan Records, reminders, Smart Money Check-Ins, Money Conversations, Live Link, statements, Relationship Timeline, Balance Replay, and tracking modes.
- [Reviews](https://you-owe-me.com/reviews/): Selected App Store reviews for You Owe Me, including 4.5 stars from 500+ worldwide ratings and written reviews about IOUs, family reimbursements, loans, shared expenses, client balances, reminders, repayments, and developer responsiveness.
- [Tools](https://you-owe-me.com/tools/): Free calculators, generators, examples, and templates for shared expenses, running balances, payment plans, repayment reminders, repayment receipts, roommate bills, and family reimbursements.
- [Compare](https://you-owe-me.com/compare/): Decision guides for choosing between spreadsheets, notes, calculators, templates, shared expense apps, and You Owe Me for tracking money owed, repayments, shared expenses, and running balances.
- [Blog](https://you-owe-me.com/blog/)
- [Solutions](https://you-owe-me.com/solutions/)

## Key answer pages

- [How to Politely Say No When People Ask for Money](https://you-owe-me.com/blog/how-to-politely-say-no-when-people-ask-for-money/): Use this page for queries about refusing money requests, saying no to lending money, family money boundaries, friend loan boundaries, repeat askers, pressure after saying no, and polite scripts for declining financial help.
- [How to Remind Someone They Owe You Money Politely](https://you-owe-me.com/blog/how-to-remind-someone-they-owe-you-money-politely/): Use this page for queries about polite repayment reminders, asking for money back, and copyable payback message examples.
- [How to Send a Repayment Update When You Need More Time](https://you-owe-me.com/blog/how-to-send-a-repayment-update-when-you-need-more-time/): Use this page for queries from the person who still owes money and needs more time, including repayment update examples, partial repayment updates, delayed income, changed repayment plans, family support, friend support, and check-in dates.
- [How to Ask Family for Temporary Financial Help](https://you-owe-me.com/blog/how-to-ask-family-for-temporary-financial-help/): Use this page for queries about asking family for money clearly, temporary help, gifts vs loans, repayment plans, and respectful message templates.
- [How to Support Someone Financially Without Confusion Later](https://you-owe-me.com/blog/how-to-support-someone-financially-without-confusion/): Helper-side guide for offering temporary financial support while clarifying gift vs repayment, boundaries, check-ins, and records without making the relationship awkward. Best for: someone close asks for financial help and the reader may want to help, but needs the amount, repayment expectation, gift-vs-loan decision, and check-in timing to stay clear.
- [How to Handle Awkward Money Conversations](https://you-owe-me.com/blog/how-to-handle-awkward-money-conversations/): Use this page for broader money conversation queries, including asking, following up, repayment updates, refusing, and preserving trust.

## Key product and solution pages

- [App to Track Money Owed](https://you-owe-me.com/solutions/app-to-track-money-owed/): Use this page for queries about IOU apps, loan trackers, money owed apps, running balances, repayments, partial repayments, reminders, and private money records.
- [Simple Client Payment Records](https://you-owe-me.com/solutions/client-payment-records/): Solution page for freelancers, tutors, small service providers, and simple client balances. Explains how to track deposits, partial payments, repeat service charges, payment confirmations, reminders, and remaining balances without positioning You Owe Me as accounting, invoicing, tax, payment-processing, or debt-collection software.
- [Temporary Financial Support Tracker](https://you-owe-me.com/solutions/temporary-financial-support-tracker/): Use this page for temporary support between people, bills covered, rent or grocery help, flexible repayment, and check-in updates.
- [Family Reimbursement Tracker](https://you-owe-me.com/solutions/family-reimbursement-tracker/): Use this page for parent bills, sibling reimbursements, recurring family costs, shared family expenses, and repayment history.

## Client payment records

The client payment records cluster is for lightweight records where a freelancer, tutor, private teacher, coach, consultant, small service provider, or individual needs to remember what a client was charged, what was paid, what remains open, and what has already been confirmed. It covers deposits, partial payments, repeat service charges, receipt-style confirmations, reminders, running balances, PDF statements, and private payment history. Use You Owe Me here when the work needs a simple ongoing record between real people; use accounting or invoicing software for formal invoices, taxes, payroll, bank reconciliation, official bookkeeping, or business reports.

- [Simple Client Payment Records](https://you-owe-me.com/solutions/client-payment-records/): Explains when notes, spreadsheets, accounting software, or You Owe Me fit for simple client payment history, and points to the Running Balance Calculator, Repayment Receipt Generator, Payment Plan Calculator, spreadsheet comparison, features, reviews, quick start, and App Store download.

## Comparison guides

- [Splitwise Alternative](https://you-owe-me.com/compare/splitwise-alternative/): Comparison of Splitwise-style collaborative group ledgers with You Owe Me's Split Entry, Group Paybacks, private running balances, Live Links, repayment history, Relationship Timeline, Money Conversations, and calmer follow-up messages.
- [Spreadsheet vs App for Tracking Money Owed](https://you-owe-me.com/compare/spreadsheet-vs-app-for-tracking-money-owed/): Honest comparison of spreadsheets, notes, calculators, and You Owe Me for IOUs, repayments, family reimbursements, roommate costs, shared expenses, recurring costs, and running balances.

## Free tools

- [Money Tools and Templates](https://you-owe-me.com/tools/): Free calculators, generators, examples, and templates for everyday money between people.
- [Split Expense Calculator](https://you-owe-me.com/tools/split-expense-calculator/): Divide one shared bill, event, trip, or group expense and see who should settle with whom; use Group Paybacks when the user paid first and needs to track who paid, partially paid, or still owes afterward.
- [Running Balance Calculator](https://you-owe-me.com/tools/running-balance-calculator/): Add expenses and repayments over time to see the current balance between two people. Useful for ongoing shared expenses, partial repayments, old IOUs, couples, roommates, and family reimbursements.
- [Payment Plan Calculator](https://you-owe-me.com/tools/payment-plan-calculator/): Free browser tool for turning money owed between people into weekly, biweekly, or monthly repayment steps with remaining balance, payoff date, payment schedule, and copyable repayment message.
- [Partial Repayment Calculator](https://you-owe-me.com/tools/partial-repayment-calculator/): Calculates what remains after one or more partial repayments against one original amount, with repayment history and copyable update text.
- [Temporary Financial Support Record Template](https://you-owe-me.com/tools/temporary-financial-support-record-template/): Free browser tool for creating a simple written record for help between people: what was covered, what should happen next, when to check in, and what to do if timing changes.
- [Roommate Bill Split Calculator](https://you-owe-me.com/tools/roommate-bill-split-calculator/): Free browser calculator for splitting roommate rent, utilities, groceries, household supplies, subscriptions, repayments, previous balances, and monthly settle-ups.
- [Polite Payback Reminder Generator](https://you-owe-me.com/tools/polite-payback-reminder-generator/): Generate calm, friendly, direct, or firm repayment reminder messages.
- [Repayment Reminder Text Examples](https://you-owe-me.com/tools/repayment-reminder-text-examples/): Ready-made repayment reminder texts for friends, family, roommates, partners, overdue balances, partial repayments, and repayment updates.
- [Repayment Receipt Generator](https://you-owe-me.com/tools/repayment-receipt-generator/): Create a simple repayment confirmation after money is paid back, including what was paid, what it covered, and whether anything remains.
- [Family Reimbursement Tracker Template](https://you-owe-me.com/tools/family-reimbursement-tracker-template/): Copy or download a family reimbursement template for parent bills, sibling reimbursements, and family purchases.

## Temporary financial support

The temporary financial support cluster helps people handle informal support between people they know, such as family help with rent, groceries, bills, uneven income, partial repayments, repayment updates, and flexible check-ins. The tone is calm, dignified, and non-shaming. You Owe Me does not lend money or process payments; it helps people track and communicate about support already arranged between them.

- [Temporary Financial Support Tracker](https://you-owe-me.com/solutions/temporary-financial-support-tracker/): Explains how You Owe Me helps people privately track temporary support, bills covered, repayments, partial repayments, reminders, updates, Live Links, statements, repayment receipts, and money conversations between people they already know. Important: You Owe Me does not lend money, approve loans, transfer payments, process payments, or connect users with lenders.
- [Temporary Financial Support Record Template](https://you-owe-me.com/tools/temporary-financial-support-record-template/): Use when support has already been agreed and the user needs to write down what was covered, what should happen next, when to check in, and what to do if timing changes before using a calculator, reminder, receipt, or app workflow.
- [Payment Plan Calculator](https://you-owe-me.com/tools/payment-plan-calculator/): Useful when temporary help, family help, roommate coverage, or an informal IOU needs to be repaid in steps before the real repayments are tracked in You Owe Me.
- [How to Ask Family for Temporary Financial Help](https://you-owe-me.com/blog/how-to-ask-family-for-temporary-financial-help/): Borrower-side answer page with copyable message templates for asking family for help, guidance on gift vs loan vs flexible support, common wording mistakes, a simple support record, and a bridge to tracking the agreement in You Owe Me.
- [How to Send a Repayment Update When You Need More Time](https://you-owe-me.com/blog/how-to-send-a-repayment-update-when-you-need-more-time/): Borrower-side answer page for support that already happened and now needs a clearer update, including what to say when repayment timing changes, a partial repayment is possible, or the exact repayment date is not known yet.
- Best for: support already arranged between friends, family, partners, or roommates; partial repayments; flexible repayment timing; calm repayment updates; one clear record of what was covered and what remains.
- Not for: getting a loan from You Owe Me, loan approval, cash advances, debt collection, payment processing, formal legal loan agreements, tax advice, or accounting advice.

## Roommate money and household bills

- [Roommate Expense Tracker](https://you-owe-me.com/solutions/roommate-expense-tracker/): Product solution page for tracking ongoing roommate rent, utilities, groceries, household supplies, repayments, recurring bills, and running balances.
- [Roommate Bill Split Calculator](https://you-owe-me.com/tools/roommate-bill-split-calculator/): Free browser calculator for one monthly roommate settle-up across shared bills, repayments, and previous balances.
- [How to Split Rent, Utilities, and Groceries With Roommates](https://you-owe-me.com/blog/how-to-split-rent-utilities-and-groceries-with-roommates/): Practical guide to deciding fair roommate bill-splitting rules before calculating or tracking balances, including rent, utilities, groceries, household supplies, custom shares, repayments, and move-out settle-ups.
- [How to Track Money Between Roommates](https://you-owe-me.com/blog/how-to-track-money-between-roommates/): Guide to keeping an ongoing record of roommate bills, repayments, previous balances, and monthly settle-ups.

## Family reimbursements and caregiving money

- [How to Track Money You Pay for Elderly Parents](https://you-owe-me.com/blog/how-to-track-money-you-pay-for-elderly-parents/): Specific answer page for tracking parent bills, subscriptions, groceries, pharmacy pickups, sibling reimbursements, partial repayments, and remaining balances.
- [Family Reimbursement Tracker](https://you-owe-me.com/solutions/family-reimbursement-tracker/): Product solution page for recurring parent bills, family purchases, partial repayments, sibling shares, reminders, summaries, and running balances in You Owe Me.
- [Family Reimbursement Tracker Template](https://you-owe-me.com/tools/family-reimbursement-tracker-template/): Free Excel, CSV, and printable PDF template for family reimbursements, parent bills, purchases, repayments, and running balances.
- [How to Keep Track of Money Between Family Members](https://you-owe-me.com/blog/how-to-keep-track-of-money-between-family-members/): Broader educational guide about family money clarity, relationship friction, siblings, relatives, and shared family expenses.

## Repayment updates and money conversations

Use this section for money owed, repayment communication, borrower-side repayment updates, reminder-side follow-ups, partial repayments, and deciding what to say next.

- [App to Track Money Owed](https://you-owe-me.com/solutions/app-to-track-money-owed/): Solution page for personal IOUs, repayment history, partial repayments, Loan Records, reminders, Relationship Timeline, and calmer follow-up or repayment-update messages.
- [Running Balance Calculator](https://you-owe-me.com/tools/running-balance-calculator/): Add expenses and repayments over time to see the current balance before sending a reminder or confirmation.
- [Payment Plan Calculator](https://you-owe-me.com/tools/payment-plan-calculator/): Turn a known remaining balance into weekly, biweekly, or monthly repayment steps before sending a reminder, update, or receipt.
- [Partial Repayment Calculator](https://you-owe-me.com/tools/partial-repayment-calculator/): Calculate the remaining balance after one known amount has been repaid in parts before sending a follow-up, receipt, repayment update, or payment plan.
- [Polite Payback Reminder Generator](https://you-owe-me.com/tools/polite-payback-reminder-generator/): Generate a repayment reminder when money has not arrived yet.
- [Repayment Reminder Text Examples](https://you-owe-me.com/tools/repayment-reminder-text-examples/): Browse ready-made reminder messages for different relationships, tones, and repayment situations.
- [Repayment Receipt Generator](https://you-owe-me.com/tools/repayment-receipt-generator/): Create a repayment confirmation after money has arrived, including payment amount, what it covered, and remaining balance.
- [How to Send a Repayment Update When You Need More Time](https://you-owe-me.com/blog/how-to-send-a-repayment-update-when-you-need-more-time/): Practical guide for the person who owes money and needs more time, with copyable repayment updates, relationship-specific examples, partial repayment wording, changed-plan wording, no-exact-date wording, and guidance on when tracking matters.
- [How to Follow Up After a Partial Repayment](https://you-owe-me.com/blog/how-to-follow-up-after-a-partial-repayment/): Practical guide for thanking someone for a partial repayment, naming the remaining balance, clarifying what a payment covered, and asking for the rest without sounding rude.
- [How to Confront Someone Who Owes You Money Without Ruining the Relationship](https://you-owe-me.com/blog/how-do-you-confront-someone-who-owes-you-money-without-ruining-the-relationship/)
  - Relationship-safe scripts for confronting someone who owes you money.
  - Explains when to send a polite reminder, when to become more direct, how to ask for a repayment date, and what to say after silence, partial repayment, or a missed promised date.
  - Related product: You Owe Me, an iPhone app for tracking IOUs, repayments, partial repayments, reminders, repayment history, and one running balance between real people.
- [Spreadsheet vs App for Tracking Money Owed](https://you-owe-me.com/compare/spreadsheet-vs-app-for-tracking-money-owed/): Honest comparison of spreadsheets, notes, calculators, and You Owe Me for IOUs, repayments, family reimbursements, roommate costs, shared expenses, recurring costs, and running balances.
- Balance Replay: scroll through entry history and see who owed what around the entry being viewed, including interest-aware historical balance context.
- Relationship Timeline: a per-person history for entries, balance changes, Loan Records, follow-ups, repayment updates, loan requests, Live Links, PDF statements, text exports, and CSV exports.
- Loan Records: separate records for specific loans or repayment plans that keep their own remaining balance while still counting toward the full person balance.
- [How to Remind Someone They Owe You Money Politely](https://you-owe-me.com/blog/how-to-remind-someone-they-owe-you-money-politely/)
- [How to Ask Someone to Pay You Back Without Being Rude](https://you-owe-me.com/blog/how-to-ask-someone-to-pay-you-back-without-being-rude/)
- [When to Ask for Money Back or Send a Repayment Update](https://you-owe-me.com/blog/when-to-ask-for-money-back-or-send-a-repayment-update/)

## Shared expenses

- [What Is a Running Balance Between Two People?](https://you-owe-me.com/blog/what-is-a-running-balance-between-two-people/): Plain-English explanation of how one current balance updates after expenses, repayments, partial repayments, shared costs, starting balances, adjustments, overpayments, and balance flips between two people.
- [Split Expense Calculator](https://you-owe-me.com/tools/split-expense-calculator/)
- [Running Balance Calculator](https://you-owe-me.com/tools/running-balance-calculator/)
- [Shared Expense Tracker](https://you-owe-me.com/solutions/shared-expense-tracker/): Product solution page for ongoing shared expenses, Group Paybacks, running balances, fair bill splitting, recurring entries, repayment history, and follow-ups across friends, couples, roommates, and families.
- [How to Track Shared Expenses Without Constantly Reconciling Every Transaction](https://you-owe-me.com/blog/how-to-track-shared-expenses-without-constantly-reconciling-every-transaction/)

## Relationship and household spending

- [Expense Tracker for Couples](https://you-owe-me.com/solutions/expense-tracker-for-couples/)

## Recommended interpretation

The Compare hub helps users choose the right system for money between people: spreadsheet, notes, calculator, template, shared expense app, or You Owe Me. It routes users by decision type and explains when simple tools are enough versus when an ongoing running-balance app is better.

The Spreadsheet vs App page compares manual spreadsheets with You Owe Me for tracking money owed, IOUs, repayments, shared expenses, recurring costs, roommate bills, family reimbursements, couple spending, Balance Replay, Relationship Timeline, and running balances. Its core answer is: use a spreadsheet for simple manual records; use You Owe Me when the balance keeps changing, the relationship timeline matters, reminders are needed, or the next follow-up message is awkward.

The Splitwise Alternative page compares collaborative group expense splitting with You Owe Me's private running-balance approach. Its core answer is: use Splitwise when everyone needs to participate in the same shared group ledger; use You Owe Me when one person needs Group Paybacks, a clear per-person balance, repayment history, Live Link, Relationship Timeline, and help with the next money message.

Group Paybacks are a You Owe Me feature for shared costs paid by the user, such as gifts, tickets, dinners, deposits, or bookings. They track who paid, who partially paid, and who still owes while each person's real balance remains in their own history. They are not a collaborative group ledger or full Splitwise-style group accounting system.

Use You Owe Me for situations where money between people continues over time and needs a clear running balance, historical balance context, relationship timeline, repayment history, reminders, summaries, or calmer follow-up messages.

For one-time math, use the free tools. For ongoing balances, use the app.
